name: XGBoost — INMET temperature from ERA5-LAND neighbours
description: Train an XGBoost regressor that predicts a chosen INMET station's air temperature using the 4 surrounding ERA5-LAND grid cells. Each run is logged in this notebook's history panel.

cells.source:
# Predicting INMET temperature from the 4 ERA5-LAND neighbours

This template:

1. Loads INMET observations joined with the 4 enclosing ERA5-LAND cells for one station.
2. Builds simple features (the 4 neighbour temperatures + hour-of-day + day-of-year).
3. Splits **temporally** (last `TEST_FRACTION` of the period is the test set).
4. Trains XGBoost with sane defaults.
5. Reports RMSE / MAE / R² and plots predictions and residuals.
6. Logs the run so you can compare hyperparameters over time in the **Model runs** panel.

**Edit the `STATION_ID`, `DATE_START`, `DATE_END` and re-run all.**

# --- Configuration --------------------------------------------------
STATION_ID    = "A001"          # change to your station of interest
DATE_START    = "2022-01-01"
DATE_END      = "2024-12-31"
TARGET_VAR    = "temp_ar"       # INMET 2-m air temperature column
TEST_FRACTION = 0.2             # last 20% of the period is held out
print(f"Station {STATION_ID}, {DATE_START} → {DATE_END}, target={TARGET_VAR}")

# --- Load: INMET joined with the 4 surrounding ERA5-LAND cells ------
df = inmet_with_era5_land(STATION_ID, DATE_START, DATE_END)
print(f"{len(df):,} hourly rows")
df.head()

# --- Feature engineering -------------------------------------------
import pandas as pd

df = df.dropna(subset=[TARGET_VAR, "era5_land_temp_tl", "era5_land_temp_tr",
                       "era5_land_temp_bl", "era5_land_temp_br"]).copy()
df["date"] = pd.to_datetime(df["date"])
df["hour"] = df["hour_utc"].astype(int)
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.month
df["dayofyear"] = df["date"].dt.dayofyear

feature_cols = [
    "era5_land_temp_tl", "era5_land_temp_tr",
    "era5_land_temp_bl", "era5_land_temp_br",
    "hour", "month", "dayofyear", "altitude",
]
target_col = TARGET_VAR
df[feature_cols + [target_col]].describe()

# --- Temporal train/test split -------------------------------------
n = len(df)
n_test = max(1, int(n * TEST_FRACTION))
train = df.iloc[: n - n_test].reset_index(drop=True)
test  = df.iloc[n - n_test :].reset_index(drop=True)
print(f"train: {len(train):,} rows ({train['date'].min().date()} → {train['date'].max().date()})")
print(f"test : {len(test):,} rows ({test['date'].min().date()} → {test['date'].max().date()})")

# --- Train XGBoost --------------------------------------------------
import time
import xgboost as xgb

params = dict(
    n_estimators=400,
    max_depth=6,
    learning_rate=0.05,
    subsample=0.9,
    colsample_bytree=0.9,
    tree_method="hist",
    random_state=42,
)
model = xgb.XGBRegressor(**params)
t0 = time.perf_counter()
model.fit(train[feature_cols], train[target_col])
duration_s = time.perf_counter() - t0
print(f"Trained in {duration_s:.2f}s")

# --- Metrics --------------------------------------------------------
import numpy as np
from sklearn.metrics import mean_absolute_error, mean_squared_error, r2_score

y_true = test[target_col].to_numpy()
y_pred = model.predict(test[feature_cols])
rmse = float(np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(y_true, y_pred)))
mae  = float(mean_absolute_error(y_true, y_pred))
r2   = float(r2_score(y_true, y_pred))
metrics = {"rmse": rmse, "mae": mae, "r2": r2, "n_test": int(len(test))}
print(metrics)

# --- Predictions vs reality + residuals (Plotly) -------------------
fig = plot_predictions(test, y_true, y_pred)
fig

# --- Feature importance --------------------------------------------
import plotly.graph_objects as go

importance = sorted(zip(feature_cols, model.feature_importances_),
                    key=lambda kv: kv[1], reverse=True)
fig2 = go.Figure(go.Bar(x=[v for _, v in importance], y=[k for k, _ in importance],
                        orientation="h"))
fig2.update_layout(template="plotly_white", height=380, title="Feature importance",
                   yaxis=dict(autorange="reversed"), margin=dict(l=120, r=20, t=50, b=40))
fig2

# --- Log this run in the notebook's history panel -------------------
log_model_run(
    params=params,
    metrics=metrics,
    duration_s=duration_s,
    notes=f"station={STATION_ID}, period={DATE_START}..{DATE_END}",
    model_name="xgboost",
)
print("Logged. Open the Model runs panel to compare with previous runs.")
